message:
fix(web): Replace ctx.scale with ctx.setTransform for HiDPI canvas rendering

Fixed a systematic bug in 11 canvas components where ctx.scale(dpr, dpr)
was used instead of ctx.setTransform(dpr, 0, 0, dpr, 0, 0). The scale()
method accumulates transforms on re-renders, causing progressive zoom/blur
on HiDPI displays when React effects re-run. setTransform() sets an
absolute transform, preventing accumulation.

Affected components:
- charts/Histogram.tsx
- charts/Sparkline.tsx
- overlays/BendabilityOverlay.tsx
- overlays/ComplexityOverlay.tsx
- overlays/GCSkewOverlay.tsx
- overlays/SelectionPressureOverlay.tsx
- overlays/TranscriptionFlowOverlay.tsx
- simulations/EvolutionVisualizer.tsx
- simulations/LysogenyVisualizer.tsx
- simulations/PlaqueVisualizer.tsx
- simulations/RibosomeVisualizer.tsx

Also fixed SimulationHub.tsx useEffect missing dependencies (orderedSims,
setOverlayData) which could cause stale closure bugs in keyboard navigation.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"phage_explorer-be3","title":"Advanced: Phage-Defense Arms Race Scanner","description":"Advanced: Integrate host defense genes (CRISPR arrays, RM, Abi) with phage anti-defense (anti-CRISPRs, Ocr-like, anti-RM) to visualize coevolutionary escalation. Shows who counters what.

**Build Plan:**
- Parse host defenses from provided host genomes/metadata
- Scan phages for anti-defense HMMs (AcrDB, Ocr-like) and methyltransferases
- Score matches (phage defense vs host counter-defense)
- Store pairs in SQLite
- Light inference in TS; heavy HMM in Rust+WASM

**Defense/Counter-Defense Pairs:**
- CRISPR ↔ Anti-CRISPR (Acr proteins)
- Type I RM ↔ Ocr/Ral methyltransferases
- Type II RM ↔ Dam/Dcm methylases
- Abi systems ↔ Anti-Abi proteins

**TUI Visualization:**
- Bipartite defense map with 'who counters what'
- Timeline bar if isolation dates known
- Meter indicating 'defense pressure' vs 'counter strength'
- Gene highlighting when hovering interactions

Novelty: 9/10, Pedagogical: 9/10, Cool Factor: 9/10.","notes":"## PHAGE-DEFENSE ARMS RACE DASHBOARD

### Concept
Summarize anti-CRISPRs, RM evasion motifs, Abi counters, retron/CBASS hits, and Sie systems; score offense/defense balance per phage.

### How to Build
- **Detection**: HMMER for anti-CRISPR families; motif scans for RM evasion; domain scans for Abi/CBASS/retron; Sie heuristics on membrane proteins
- **Scoring**: Weighted offense/defense index; store with gene links
- **Cross-phage compare**: Rank phages by offense/defense; show missing counters

### Why It's Good
Offers an immediate strategic view of how a phage deals with host defenses; guides cocktail design.

### Implementation Stack
HMMER precompute; TS scoring; SQLite cache; Ink radar/stacked bars; all CPU, no GPU.

### TUI Visualization
Radar/stacked bars for offense vs defense; clicking a wedge jumps to the gene; "missing counter" badges with suggestions.

### Files
- packages/core/src/analysis/arms-race.ts

 {"id":"phage_explorer-bjoz","title":"Phase 3: Analysis Overlays","description":"## Overview

Implement all 31 analysis overlay types that exist in the TUI, transforming ASCII visualizations into rich Canvas/WebGL graphics.

## Why This Phase Matters

Analysis overlays are the **core value proposition** of Phage Explorer. They transform raw sequence data into biological insights through:
- Statistical analysis (GC skew, complexity, k-mer anomalies)
- Structural predictions (bendability, promoters, non-B DNA)
- Evolutionary signals (HGT, selection pressure, codon bias)
- Visual patterns (dot plots, CGR, Hilbert curves)

Without these, the app is just a sequence viewer.

## Current State

### Tier 1: Already Started (8 overlays)
These exist in web but may need completion/enhancement:
- ✅ GCSkewOverlay - GC skew across genome
- ✅ ComplexityOverlay - Entropy/complexity analysis
- ✅ BendabilityOverlay - DNA bendability profile
- ✅ PromoterOverlay - Promoter site predictions
- ✅ RepeatsOverlay - Repeat finder visualization
- ✅ KmerAnomalyOverlay - K-mer anomaly heatmap
- ✅ ModuleOverlay - Functional module mapping
- ✅ TranscriptionFlowOverlay - Expression program flow

### Tier 2: Missing (17 overlays)
These need to be created from scratch:
- PackagingPressureOverlay - DNA packaging constraints
- VirionStabilityOverlay - Virion structural stability
- PhasePortraitOverlay - AA property PCA scatter
- BiasDecompositionOverlay - Codon bias PCA
- HGTOverlay - Horizontal gene transfer islands
- CRISPROverlay - CRISPR spacer mapping
- SyntenyOverlay - Gene order conservation
- TropismOverlay - Host receptor predictions
- StructureConstraintOverlay - RNA structure constraints
- NonBDNAOverlay - G-quadruplex, Z-DNA, cruciform
- AnomalyOverlay - Statistical anomaly detection
- DotPlotOverlay - Self-similarity matrix
- CGROverlay - Chaos Game Representation
- HilbertOverlay - Hilbert curve visualization
- GelOverlay - Virtual gel electrophoresis

### Supporting Components (3 additional)
- AAKeyOverlay - Amino acid color reference
- AALegend - Compact AA legend
- SearchOverlay - Phage search interface

## Architecture Pattern

All overlays follow a consistent pattern:

```typescript
export function [Name]Overlay() {
  // 1. Get sequence from store
  const sequence = usePhageStore(s => s.currentPhage?.sequence ?? '');
  
  // 2. Get or compute analysis data (via worker)
  const data = useAnalysisResult('[name]', sequence);
  
  // 3. Render with standard overlay wrapper
  return (
    <Overlay title="[Name] Analysis" onClose={...}>
      <AnalysisCanvas data={data} />
      <StatisticsPanel data={data} />
    </Overlay>
  );
}
```

## Visualization Upgrades (ASCII → Canvas)

| TUI ASCII | Web Canvas | Enhancement |
|-----------|-----------|-------------|
| Text sparklines | Sparkline.tsx | Smooth curves, hover values |
| ASCII heatmaps | HeatmapCanvas | Color gradients, zoom |
| Text scatter plots | ScatterCanvas | Interactive PCA exploration |
| Box-drawing charts | SVG/Canvas | Animation, interactivity |
| ASCII dot matrix | Canvas ImageData | Full resolution |

## Dependencies

- **Depends on**: Phase 1 (database), Phase 2 (core views for context)
- **Blocks**: None directly, but needed for feature parity

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All 8 Tier 1 overlays fully functional
- [ ] All 17 Tier 2 overlays implemented
- [ ] Each overlay has Canvas-based visualization
- [ ] Analysis runs in Web Worker (non-blocking)
- [ ] Loading states while computing
- [ ] Can trigger overlays via keyboard shortcuts
- [ ] Menu system lists all available overlays

## Files to Create

```
packages/web/src/components/overlays/
├── PackagingPressureOverlay.tsx
├── VirionStabilityOverlay.tsx
├── PhasePortraitOverlay.tsx
├── BiasDecompositionOverlay.tsx
├── HGTOverlay.tsx
├── CRISPROverlay.tsx
├── SyntenyOverlay.tsx
├── TropismOverlay.tsx
├── StructureConstraintOverlay.tsx
├── NonBDNAOverlay.tsx
├── AnomalyOverlay.tsx
├── DotPlotOverlay.tsx
├── CGROverlay.tsx
├── HilbertOverlay.tsx
├── GelOverlay.tsx
├── SearchOverlay.tsx
├── AAKeyOverlay.tsx
└── AALegend.tsx
```

## Estimated Complexity: High

This is the largest phase by file count. However, each overlay follows a similar pattern, making it parallelizable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-08T12:54:57.593613-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-08T12:54:57.593613-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-bjoz","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-qiaq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-08T12:55:22.720971-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

-{"id":"phage_explorer-owy","title":"Layer 2: Host Range Predictor (CRISPR Spacer Matching)","description":"Fuse CRISPR spacer hits with receptor-binding motif classifiers. Score per host lineage with evidence (spacer alignments, RBP motif hits). MinHash/LSH index for spacers + HMMER for RBP families. Bayesian fusion scoring. Evidence table per host with strict/permissive thresholds, host cloud sized by probability. Novelty: High, Pedagogical: High.","notes":"## HOST-RANGE INFERENCE VIA CRISPR SPACERS + RECEPTOR MOTIFS

**Full Implementation from Roadmap**

### Concept
Fuse CRISPR spacer hits with receptor-binding motif classifiers to predict host range. Score per host lineage and display evidence.

### How to Build

**Spacer DB Ingest:**
- Parse spacer FASTAs/TSVs from CRISPRdb, IMG/VR, CRISPR-Cas++
- Build MinHash/LSH index (Rust+WASM) for fast lookup
- Fall back to banded alignments for top hits
- Store spacer→host mappings with confidence

**Motif/RBP Typing:**
- HMMER precompute for RBP families (beta-helix, depolymerase, etc.)
- TS classifier on receptor-binding domain signatures
- Link RBP domains to known receptors (LPS, OmpA, FhuA, etc.)

**Fusion Scoring:**
```
log P(host | spacer, motif) = w₁ × spacer_score + w₂ × motif_score + prior
Confidence = σ(log_odds)
```

**Evidence Types:**
- Spacer alignments with mismatches
- RBP domain hits with E-values
- Known receptor associations

### TUI Features
- Host leaderboard with confidence bars
- Press host to see spacer hit list and RBP motif hits
- 'Host cloud' ASCII plot sized by probability
- Toggle 'strict vs permissive' thresholds
- Evidence drilldown per candidate

### Why It's Good
- Combines complementary evidence types
- Tightens weak host predictions
- Points to mechanisms (which receptor, which spacer)
- Guides experimental validation

### Implementation Stack
- Rust+WASM LSH for spacer queries
- HMMER precompute for RBP domains
- Fusion/scoring in TS
- SQLite cache for hits and scores
- Ink UI tables and plots

Novelty: 9/10, Pedagogical: 9/10, Wow Factor: 8/10","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:12:45.734-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T13:25:18.704593-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-owy","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-54g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:12:45.735205-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-owy","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-2g5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:39:32.636107-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

```
log P(host | spacer, motif) = w₁ × spacer_score + w₂ × motif_score + prior
```

- Guides experimental validation

 {"id":"phage_explorer-wae","title":"Advanced: Pan-Phage Latent Space Atlas","description":"Layer 2: Explore a 'phage universe' using genome embeddings that capture composition, motifs, and structural signals. Learn fixed-length vectors for phage genomes via contrastive encoders (BYOL/SimCLR-style), enabling fast nearest-neighbor search beyond ANI/k-mers.

**Build Plan:**
- Train/finetune contrastive encoder on large phage + host-viral corpora
- Core in Python (PyTorch + bitsandbytes), export to ONNX
- Run inference in Bun via ONNX Runtime Web or WASM
- Precompute embeddings for bundled phages, cache to SQLite
- Use UMAP/t-SNE (Rust+WASM via umap-js/tsne-rs) for on-device projection

**Why It's Good:**
- Captures higher-order signals (motif co-occurrence, periodicity, codon bias)
- Enables 'find me things like this' beyond simple k-mer overlap
- Shows latent space vs classic metrics (ANI, Mash)

**TUI Visualization:**
- Interactive 2D 'phage universe' scatter with density shading
- Keyboard navigation to jump to nearest neighbors
- Press 'l' to recenter on current phage with animated zoom
- Hover tooltips show phage metadata

Novelty: 9/10, Pedagogical: 9/10, Cool Factor: 10/10.","notes":"## PAN-PHAGE LATENT SPACE ATLAS (LLM-AUGMENTED)

**Full Implementation from Roadmap**

### Concept
Learn joint embeddings for genomes and proteomes using k-mer sketches plus protein LM embeddings (ESM/ProtT5). Cluster phages by functional/evolutionary proximity; explain cluster membership with 'semantic diffs.'

### How to Build

**Embeddings:**
- TS pipeline for k-mer MinHash sketches
- Python/Rust sidecar for ESM2/ProtT5 protein embeddings (tail fibers, polymerases, repressors)
- Cache vectors in SQLite/Parquet

**Fusion:**
- Concatenate or project via PCA/UMAP
- Rust+WASM or TS (ml-js) for projection to 2D/3D
- HNSW neighbor index (wasm-hnsw or Rust HNSW)

**Explanations:**
- For neighbor pairs, surface:
  - Top k-mer deltas
  - Domain differences
  - Codon/AA usage deltas

### TUI Features
- ASCII UMAP scatterplot with live cursor
- Select point to list nearest neighbors and 'why' (top domains/k-mers)
- Glow outliers with novelty scores
- Toggle layers: genome-only vs protein-LLM fused
- [L] Recenter on current phage with animated zoom
- Hover tooltips with phage metadata
- Density shading for cluster visualization

### Why It's Good
- Maps 'phage space' blending sequence + functional signals
- Fast neighbor lookup enables 'find similar' queries
- Highlights outliers and novelty
- Shows where different molecular layers agree or disagree

### Implementation Stack
- MinHash in TS
- Embeddings via optional Python microservice (ESM2) cached to disk
- HNSW in Rust+WASM for fast kNN
- UMAP in Rust (umap-rs) or TS (umap-js)
- Ink UI for interactive scatter

Novelty: 9/10, Pedagogical: 9/10, Wow Factor: 10/10","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:19:10.733099-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T13:22:00.720674-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-wae","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-2g5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:19:10.736323-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-wae","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-54g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-06T23:19:10.738549-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
